title: Layerwise Coupling Mechanism in 7B+ Base vs Reasoning-Tuned Transformers
hypothesis: The surgery x jitter interaction detected at 7B+ in exp-014/015 is layer-localized in mid/late layers of base transformers, and reasoning training regularizes/compresses this vulnerable band. Local representation geometry (PR, anisotropy) at each layer predicts interaction magnitude.
value_to_investors: Determines whether the transformer-specific coupling from exp-015 is a shallow surface effect (layer-specific, geometry-predictable) or a deep architectural property. If layer-localized, deployment testing can target specific layers. If reasoning training eliminates it, reasoning-tuned models need less validation.
value_to_researchers: First layerwise decomposition of the surgery-jitter interaction at 7B+ scale. Tests three mechanistic hypotheses (layer localization, training regularization, geometric prediction) with 32,000 factorial trials plus clean activation geometry. Connects causal perturbation effects to representation structure.
identification_strategy: Same 2x2 factorial as exp-014/015 (surgery=0.08, jitter=0.08) applied at 5 layer positions [0.20, 0.35, 0.50, 0.65, 0.80]. 5 cached models grouped as base_transformer (3) vs reasoning_tuned (2). Phase 1: 32,000 eval trials. Phase 2: clean geometry pass (128 prompts, compute PR and anisotropy per layer). Interaction computed per model per layer. Group comparison via Wilcoxon signed-rank. Geometry-interaction correlation via Pearson/Spearman + multiple regression.
falsification_tests: If base transformers show uniform interaction across all layers (no localization), H1 is falsified
If reasoning models show comparable interaction to base at any layer, H2 is falsified
If PR and anisotropy show no significant correlation with interaction (|r| < 0.3), H3 is falsified
If calibration-holdout gap > 5pp, prompt-level overfitting is present
success_criteria: All 5 models complete (all cached, no downloads needed)
Clear verdict: TRAINING_REGULARIZATION, GEOMETRIC_BOTTLENECK, or NO_CLEAR_MECHANISM
At least one hypothesis supported with p < 0.05
Geometry data collected at all 25 model-layer positions

data_lineage.source: HuggingFace Hub models (all pre-cached from exp-014/015)
data_lineage.provenance: 5 models at 7B+ (3 base transformer, 2 reasoning-tuned transformer), NF4 quantized via bitsandbytes on RTX 5090 Laptop (23.9GB VRAM). All models cached locally from prior experiments.
data_lineage.transformations: Phase 1: Forward pass with hook-based hidden-state perturbation at 5 layer positions, greedy decoding (max_new_tokens=10), strict answer parsing. Phase 2: Clean forward pass with activation capture hooks, PR via eigenvalue decomposition, anisotropy via cosine similarity.

out_of_sample_validation_plan: 32 calibration prompts used for analysis tuning; 32 holdout prompts reserved for final validation. Interaction computed on both splits with divergence check.
uncertainty_methods: Bootstrap CIs (5000 resamples) per layer position
Permutation null distribution (5000 shuffles of surgery/jitter labels within model)
Pearson and Spearman correlations for geometry-interaction link
robustness_checks: Per-model interaction profiles (individual trajectories vs group means)
Main effects (surgery, jitter) by layer position
H1: t-test comparing mid/late vs early/extreme layers in base transformers
H2: Wilcoxon signed-rank test on paired layer-level interactions
H3: Pearson + Spearman correlations, group-separated correlations
Mixed-effects model with layer x group x interaction terms
